Product Id: A residential property" refers to all land and structures intended for private occupancy
CANSIM Id: whether on a permanent or a temporary basis."

Product Id: The geographic boundaries used in this table are the 2016 census metropolitan areas boundaries.

A sale" of residential property refers to whether a property had a transfer of ownership from one party to another party through a contractual agreement. A property is considered to have been sold on the date that the property was transferred to
URL: registered in or otherwise carried in the name of the party who purchased the property. More than one property may be sold in the same contractual agreement. Pre-sales are not included."

Product Id: A sale type" of residential property refers to whether a property was sold in a market or non-market sale. "Market sale" refers to an arm’s length transaction where all parties act independently with no influence over the other. "Non-market sale" refers to non-arm’s length transactions
A buyer" refers to an individual or non-individual that has the property title transferred to
URL: registered in or otherwise carried in their name from a different party."

Product Id: Sale price" of residential property refers to the dollar amount set forth during the sale of the property as per the contractual agreement."
Property type" refers to property characteristics and/or dwelling configuration
CANSIM Id: on which there can be one or more residential structures. Property types include: single-detached houses

Product Id: A condominium apartment" refers to a set of living quarters that is owned individually
CANSIM Id: while land and common elements are held in joint ownership with others."

Product Id: A mobile home" is a single dwelling
CANSIM Id: designed and constructed to be transported on its own chassis and capable of being moved to a new location on short notice. It may be placed temporarily on a foundation pad and may be covered by a skirt. This category may also include houseboats and mini homes."

Product Id: An other property type" refers to a property where the structure has a single dwelling unit in a mixed-use building and does not fall into any of the other categories
CANSIM Id: or where the structure type is unknown."

Product Id: A property with multiple residential units" refers to a property containing more than one set of living quarters owned by the same owner(s)
CANSIM Id: as is the case for an apartment building or a duplex."

Product Id: Vacant land" refers to a property on which there are currently no residential structures
CANSIM Id: but where regulations permit that one or more residential structures can be built. Vacant land may also include land on which an existing residential structure has not yet been assessed or land on which a residential structure is present
URL: but owned by another entity (for example a mobile home in a mobile home park)."

Product Id: Period of construction" refers to the period in time during which the building or dwelling was originally constructed. This refers to the period in which the building was completed
CANSIM Id: not the time of any later remodelling
URL: additions or conversions. For properties having multiple residential structures
Cube Notes: this refers to the period in which the most recent structure was completed."

Product Id: Counts undergo random rounding, a process that transforms all raw counts into randomly rounded counts. This reduces the possibility of identifying individuals in the tabulations. All percentages are derived from rounded counts, subtotals and totals may not exactly equal the sum of components due to system rounding.
Averages and medians are calculated using properties with non-missing or non-zero values when there is no imputation available for the variables of interest (for example sale price).
Estimates of sale prices are not available for non-market sales.

The universe of this table is restricted to the residential properties sold from January 1 to December 31 of the reference year, whereas the information on the property characteristics (property types and periods of construction) is from the following reference year.
